## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hello, in this video I'm going to walk you through how to quickly post an IGTV video to your Instagram page by using Facebook Creator Studio for Instagram. Before starting, quick note, just make sure your video assets have already been appropriately resized. Your IGTV video should have an aspect ratio of 9 to 16 and needs to be an MP4 format. So if you plan on posting your IGTV video to your Instagram grid as well, you can add a cover photo when uploading your video. I've already resized our photo and video assets using InShot so we are ready to go. First, just want to open up Creator Studio. Now once you've logged in, select the Instagram icon at the top of the page. Now once you're on the Instagram section of Creator Studio, you can then create a post by selecting the green post icon. This will give you the option to either create a post for your Instagram feed or IGTV. In this case we are making an IGTV post. Now we're going to select IGTV and from here you'll be able to upload your content as well as a title and description to your post. First, go up with this. And while that's uploading, I'm going to quickly pull up and enter the copy I want to use for this video. Alright, so now we have our title. Now we just want to add a description for our video. Okay, great. After everything has been entered and looks okay, you can then select a cover image for your video. Creator Studio will generate a bunch of thumbnails for you, but we would like to add a custom image here. I've already created a cover for the video. Your custom cover image should also be the same size as the IGTV video. If you are uploading your IGTV video to your Instagram grid as well, just note that viewers will only see an image with a one to one aspect ratio. Also just be sure to edit your photo accordingly.

## [03:01 - 04:20]

Once your custom image has been uploaded, you can then just take a look back at your post info, double check that everything is good to go. And yes, we do want to share a preview of this to our feed, so make sure that that is selected. And from there, you are ready to publish. With Creator Studio, you do have the option of publishing immediately. You can also schedule your post for a later date and time, or you can just save it as a draft, but for all intents and purposes, we are going to publish this now. So just give me one moment while this uploads. Once Creator Studio has finished publishing your post, you should see this as published under post status. And then to make sure that everything has uploaded correctly, you can head over to Instagram just to make sure that everything is there. So give me one moment while that opens up. You can select the IGTV section here, just scroll down a bit, and it looks like it's uploaded. Alright, so I hope this video was helpful and let me know if there are any other questions that I can answer for you.
